Applicant.title: Applicant
Applicant.description: Stores personal information of an individual applying for a job through the platform. This data is collected during the application process.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Applicant entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the applicant.

phoneNumber.description: The applicant's phone number, starting with '254' and without a plus sign. Example: 254704118070.

county.description: The county where the applicant resides.

ward.description: The ward where the applicant resides.

highestEducationQualification.description: The highest education level attained by the applicant. Options: Primary, Secondary, College, University.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the applicant's initial information was submitted.

JobPosting.title: JobPosting
JobPosting.description: Represents a specific job position available for application, detailing its requirements, general benefits, and application timeline within the Canada recruitment drive.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobPosting entity.

title.description: The official title of the job position (e.g., 'Accounting Clerk', 'Construction Laborer').

generalDescription.description: A general description of the Canada recruitment drive and overall context, common to many job postings.

minimumAge.description: The minimum age requirement for applicants (e.g., 18).

languageRequirement.description: Description of language proficiency required (e.g., 'Be conversant English').

annualSalaryMin.description: The minimum annual salary in Canadian Dollars (e.g., 69500).

annualSalaryCurrency.description: The currency of the annual salary (e.g., 'CAD').

applicationDeadline.description: The final date and time for submitting applications for this job posting.

jobOfferLetterDate.description: The date when job offer letters will be issued for successful applicants.

commonBenefitsDescription.description: A detailed description of the common benefits offered to all successful applicants (e.g., 'Free Kenya Passport Application', 'Fully funded scholarships in Canada').

Application.title: Application
Application.description: Represents a formal job application submitted by an applicant, linking their personal details to a specific job posting and managing its lifecycle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Application entity.

applicantId.description: Reference to the Applicant who submitted this application. (Relationship: Applicant 1:N Application)

jobPostingId.description: Reference to the specific JobPosting the applicant is applying for. (Relationship: JobPosting 1:N Application)

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the application was formally submitted by the applicant.

status.description: The current status of the application, tracking its progress through the recruitment pipeline. Options: Pending Payment, Payment Verified, Under Review, Interview Scheduled, Offer Issued, Rejected.

adminNotes.description: Internal notes added by an administrator or HR regarding the application.

PaymentTransaction.title: PaymentTransaction
PaymentTransaction.description: Stores the details of an M-Pesa payment made by an applicant for the registration fee, enabling verification and ensuring the uniqueness of transaction codes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentTransaction entity.

mpesaTransactionCode.description: The unique M-Pesa transaction code provided by the applicant. This code must be unique across all recorded transactions to prevent reuse.

applicantId.description: Reference to the Applicant who made this payment. (Relationship: Applicant 1:N PaymentTransaction)

applicationId.description: Reference to the specific Application this payment is for. (Relationship: Application 1:1 PaymentTransaction)

amountPaid.description: The amount of the registration fee paid by the applicant (expected to be Ksh. 950).

paymentDateReported.description: The date and time when the applicant reported the M-Pesa transaction code.

verificationStatus.description: The status of the M-Pesa transaction code verification (e.g., 'Pending', 'Verified', 'Invalid Code', 'Duplicate Code', 'Amount Mismatch').

verifiedByAdminId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the administrator who performed the verification. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N PaymentTransaction)

verificationDate.description: The date and time when the payment transaction was verified by an administrator.

definition.description: Collection of all available job postings. Publicly readable for all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to administrators only. `jobPostingId` refers to the unique identifier for each job posting.

params.name: jobPostingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the JobPosting.

definition.description: Each authenticated user (`userId`) has one `applicant_profile` document storing their personal details for job applications. This structure enforces private, user-owned data where `userId` from the path must match `request.auth.uid`. The `id` field within the document must also match `userId`. This simplifies ownership-based security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase User ID (UID) of the authenticated user who owns this profile.

definition.description: Stores individual job applications submitted by a specific user. `userId` in the path ensures that only the owning user or an administrator can access their applications. The document itself contains `applicantId` (matching `userId` from the path) and `jobPostingId` for authorization independence and contextual linking without `get()` calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase User ID (UID) of the applicant who submitted this application.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the application.

definition.description: Globally stores M-Pesa payment transaction details, using `mpesaTransactionCode` as the document ID to ensure global uniqueness and prevent duplicate usage. Contains denormalized `applicantId` (the payer) and `applicationId` (the application it's for) to support authorization rules without external lookups. Applicants can create their own transactions, and administrators can verify them.

params.name: mpesaTransactionCode
params.description: The unique M-Pesa transaction code, used as the document ID for uniqueness enforcement.

definition.description: A collection for Database Access Control (DBAC). Each document in this collection represents an administrator, where the document ID (`adminId`) is the Firebase User ID (UID) of the administrator. Used to check if `request.auth.uid` corresponds to an admin, enabling secure access for administrative tasks. (Implicit 'Admin' entity for role management, schema is minimal with 'id' field matching 'adminId').

params.name: adminId
params.description: The Firebase User ID (UID) of the administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access control by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) via Denormalization:**

1.  **`Applicant` Data (`/users/{userId}/applicant_profile`):** Each applicant's profile data (`Applicant` entity) is stored directly under their authenticated user ID (`userId`). This means the `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, making ownership explicit and rules atomic. The `applicantId` field within the document will also be set to `request.auth.uid`, ensuring consistency. No `get()` calls are needed to determine who owns the profile.
2.  **`Application` Data (`/users/{userId}/applications/{applicationId}`):** Similar to the applicant profile, each `Application` document is nested under the owning `userId`. The document itself contains `applicantId` (which matches the `userId` in the path) and `jobPostingId`. This denormalization means that rules for reading, updating, or deleting an application can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` in the path segment or the `applicantId` field within the document, without needing to fetch parent documents. This enables atomic operations and simplifies rule logic.
3.  **`PaymentTransaction` Data (`/payment_transactions/{mpesaTransactionCode}`):** To enforce the crucial requirement that "one mpesa message should not be used twice," the `mpesaTransactionCode` is used as the document ID for `PaymentTransaction`. The document also denormalizes `applicantId` and `applicationId`. This allows a security rule for `create` operations to check for the global uniqueness of the `mpesaTransactionCode` using `exists()`, preventing duplicate codes and avoiding `get()` calls on potentially related application or applicant documents for authorization decisions. Authorization for an applicant to create or read their own transaction can be based on `applicantId` within the document, which must match `request.auth.uid`.
4.  **`Admin` Roles (`/admins/{adminId}`):** A dedicated top-level collection `admins` is used, where each document ID (`adminId`) is an administrator's Firebase UID. Authorization for administrative actions (e.g., verifying payments, managing job postings) is achieved by checking for the existence of `request.auth.uid` in this collection, (`exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admins/$(request.auth.uid))`). This completely decouples role checking from other data, making it robust and easy to debug.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

1.  **`JobPosting` (`/job_postings/{jobPostingId}`):** These are publicly readable for all authenticated users, meaning `list` operations on this collection are inherently secure as all documents share the same access posture. Write operations are restricted to `admins`.
2.  **`Applicant` Profile (`/users/{userId}/applicant_profile`):** Access is strictly path-based, meaning `request.auth.uid` must match `userId`. A `list` operation on `/users/{request.auth.uid}/applicant_profile` is secure as it explicitly scopes to the authenticated user's data.
3.  **`Application` Data (`/users/{userId}/applications/{applicationId}`):** Similar to applicant profiles, `list` operations are scoped to the authenticated user's `userId`. This ensures that an applicant can only list their own applications, preventing accidental data exposure.
4.  **`PaymentTransaction` (`/payment_transactions/{mpesaTransactionCode}`):** While `create` operations enforce uniqueness via the document ID, `list` operations for non-admins would be denied by default, or could be allowed with strict filters (`.where('applicantId', '==', request.auth.uid)` if needed, but usually, specific `get` access is sufficient for the submitting user). Admin `list` access would be permitted to facilitate verification. The primary security challenge (unique `mpesaTransactionCode`) is handled by the ID structure and `create` rule, not by `list` filtering.
